comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
EXPERIMENTAL RESULTS (round 4) — decomposition review + DSO full-suite + overlay triggering + signal-loss study

Ran the full review suite on this epic + its 9 children, on a varied 12-ticket DSO sample, validated overlay
triggering, and compared our finding volume to DSO's fidelity review. ~110 LLM runs. Full writeup:
docs/experiments/plan-review-decomposition-overlays-retune.md; raw data under docs/experiments/data/ (r4_*.jsonl).

A) EPIC + CHILDREN: the epic is clean (4 minor findings; G5 decomposition RESOLVED now it has children). But the
   9 child STORIES drew 7-10 mostly-MAJOR findings each — the gate applied full LEAF-implementation scrutiny (E6
   proving-commands, leaf overlays) to mid-level stories that defer that detail to their tasks. -> the round's
   central tuning signal.

B) DSO SAMPLE (12: epics incl. one without brainstorm:complete, stories, tasks, bugs): the new EXP criterion
   behaves correctly (suppressed on the bc47 pilot epic + all mechanical work; fired only on 1414's underived
   batch=20 threshold). Bugs drew 6-7 planning-findings each -> empirically confirms the design's bug-exemption.
   E5/E6/F4/overlays over-fire on mechanical/test tasks (TYPE mismatch).

C) OVERLAY TRIGGERING validated: T1/T5a/T5d fire from low-FP DETERMINISTIC signals (bright-lines/UI-keyword,
   12-13/13 agreement, ~0 FP); T8/T6/T5b/T5c/T9 must be LLM-ROUTED (T8 keyword-matches 9/13 but only 3 relevant ->
   high FP). Encoded per-overlay in the registry trigger field.

D) SIGNAL-LOSS (why we are leaner than DSO): NOT losing signal. On b575 DSO recorded 12 findings, our single-turn
   7 — but our T8 agentic structural-gap probe RECOVERED 5 of DSO's 6 bot-psychologist gaps (same enum-vocabulary-
   mismatch class). DSO's extra volume on e7f3 (8 vs ours) was forced per-dimension scoring (3->5 revisions) our
   anti-FP coaching posture correctly drops. The only signal-loss risk was UNDER-TRIGGERING T8 — fixed by the
   overlay router firing T8 on LLM/agent-design plans. Our leanness = less noise, same signal.

RE-TUNE (implemented + re-run): proportionate scrutiny by LEVEL (epic/story/task) + TYPE (bug-exempt; E5-suppress
on test tasks; F4 N/A on internal leaf), via an `applies_at` criterion field. Re-run validation: children dropped
7-10 -> 3-8 findings, concentrated on the REAL ones (E2 ambiguities, E5 testing-strategy, G5 decompose-into-tasks,
EXP unvalidated claims, T5e arch); leaf-noise gone. Criteria content is well-calibrated; the fix is ROUTING + the
one new EXP criterion. Epic design + the overlays/layer2 child stories updated accordingly.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
=== ROUND 5: criteria coverage + 2025-26 research + senior-reviewer gaps + DSO decider + alternatives design + critical review ===

Answered Joe's coverage/gaps questions with 5 xhigh subagents (Google PSE criteria, Principal-SWE gap audit,
cited 2025-26 LLM-plan failure-mode research, broad critical review of our plan, DSO implementation-plan decider
extraction) + direct analysis. Full writeup: docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-coverage-and-gaps.md. Complete
LLM registry now docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v6.json (31 descriptors), guard updated+passing.

COVERAGE (Q1): 8/10 named dims already well-covered (maintainability T5e, reliability T5b, perf T5a, security T5c,
a11y T5d, feasibility T3/E4, fitness F4/E3/E6, accuracy-vs-code E4/G1G2/A1). Two attention points: CORRECTNESS-of-
APPROACH was a real gap (a well-formed plan can propose a wrong design, e.g. check-then-act idempotency=TOCTOU) ->
new G6; architectural decay is plan-level only (P4/A1/T5e); longitudinal decay is retro/out-of-scope.

RESEARCH (Q2): 15-mode taxonomy (~30 cites). Most-compounding for autonomous agents = early/silent/path-dependent
(#1 unverified assumptions, #2 premature-commitment, #5 ambiguity). #1/#5 well-covered; #2 GENERAL CASE is the key
strategic partial-gap -> G6. Clean misses: #3 weighing-alternatives -> G6; #15 self-verification-trust -> handled
by architecture. Partials tightened: #8 change-detector tests -> E5; #12 rollback-as-remedy -> T4 + T11/T12.

ADDITIONS (disciplined distinct-vs-rollin + valuable+generic): ADD 4 distinct -> G6 approach-soundness/anti-patterns/
alternative-selection (AGENT, the top gap; subsumes correctness+alternatives+fitness+the 7 DSO anti-patterns),
T10 infrastructure/IaC (Joe-requested; deterministic trigger), T11 data-migration/backfill safety, T12 rollout/
rollback/reversibility. ROLL-IN 8 (A1+=7 anti-patterns, T9+=concurrency/TOCTOU, T5a+=cost, T5b+=observability/
degraded-mode, T5c+=least-priv/secrets, E5+=change-detector, T4+=rollback-remedy, P3+=license/CVE, G5 consume-P4 +
vertical-slice MVP sequencing). Redundancy notes: E1 vs COH boundary, E2 vs E6 dedup, G5 mis-tier.

DSO DECIDER (Q7): approach-decision-maker (5 dims: codebase-alignment/blast-radius/testability/simplicity/robustness
+ 7 anti-patterns) + approach-proposer (>=3 distinct on 4 axes) -> folded into G6. KEY: DSO validates our anti-priming
design - its task-decomposer gets ONLY the chosen approach ("do not re-architect"); rejected alternatives are
logged in-session, NEVER persisted to a ticket the implementer reads.

ALTERNATIVES WITHOUT NEGATIVE PRIMING (Q8): G6 separates process (were alternatives weighed?) from the implementer-
facing plan (chosen approach + POSITIVE rationale only). Reviewer GENERATES 1-2 structural alternatives, checks the
chosen is defensible, then DISCARDS them; a clearly-better miss -> coaching to the PLANNER; consideration recorded
in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar. Zero rejected options in the implementer's artifact.

CRITICAL REVIEW -> WHICH CRITERION SHOULD CATCH IT (Q9): self-validating - the two CRITICAL findings map to COH (a
criterion we'd DROPPED) and G6 (the one we ADDED). #1 blocking-on-claim-contradicts-coaching -> COH+E3; #2 hollow
signal (fail-open+advisory+--force certifies skipped/ignored) -> G6+E6; #3 Jaccard-not-ground-truth -> T2+F1;
#4 50ms-sleight + cache-busts-on-re-review -> T5a; #5 no-thin-slice-MVP -> G5 sequencing. DESIGN-LEVEL findings
flagged to Joe as OPEN QUESTIONS on the epic: blocking-on-claim vs coaching; what-the-signature-certifies (richer
rigor vector + CI-side gating). Joe's call - the brainstorm deliberately chose claim as the enforcement point.

BOOLEAN/CHECKLIST (Q10): YES, deliberate (v3 §1 binary-checklist over scalar; CheckEval/TICKing). Criteria authored
as binary (a)/(b)/(c) sub-checks; nuances: structured checklist[] field not yet populated (prose, mechanical to
lift); genuinely-qualitative (E3 restate, BROAD) correctly stay narrative - boolean where viable without losing signal.

Updated epic 5fd2 (authoritative list += G6/T10/T11/T12 + roll-in note + open-design-questions), layer2 (+G6) +
overlays (+T10/T11/T12) children. All pass check_ac + clarity. NEXT: lift prose sub-checks into structured
checklist[]; decide the blocking/CI posture; exercise the never-run criteria (G6/T10/T11/T12/T1/T3/T4/T5d/G3/G4).

resolves them.

2. ROUTE the codebase-grounded criteria (G6/T10/T11/T9/COH/A1/E4/G1G2) + T3 to the AGENT tier (where AMBIGUOUS
   resolves to a concrete verdict); single-turn is for the non-grounded judgment criteria.
3. RUN E4 — generalization on a NON-DSO corpus (rebar's own tickets / other styles). MUST-HAVE before claiming the
   defaults/routing generalize: all validation so far used DSO data, the same population the applies_at routing was
   tuned on. (Experiment plan: docs/experiments/plan-review-validation-experiment-plan.md, E2-E7.)
4. LIFT the prose binary sub-checks into the structured `checklist[]` descriptor field (registry + layer2 child).
5. HARDEN structured-output parsing (one T5d run returned an empty finding).

THEN: file the F1-F12 follow-on register as separate discovered_from-linked tickets; begin implementation per the
9 children (registry/orchestrator first — it's the dependency root).

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
Plan-review criteria must explicitly catch two antipatterns (both surfaced repeatedly in our own LLM-code-review design work):

1. "Reinventing the wheel" — proposing custom-built machinery for a problem that mature, actively-maintained tools/standards already solve (e.g. inventing content-digest review provenance when branch protection + a merge gate + Gerrit submitWholeTopic already solve "all code on main was reviewed"). The gate should ask: does an established, well-trodden solution already exist? If so, the plan must justify NOT adopting it.

2. "Frankenstein solution" — stitching together an ad-hoc amalgam of mismatched parts when a single coherent, demonstrated-to-work design exists (e.g. blending Google + Anthropic + Conventional Comments severity schemes instead of adopting one proven output contract end-to-end). The gate should ask: is this a coherent, demonstrated pairing, or a custom composite? Prefer the proven pairing.

Make both first-class checks in the plan-review rubric, not implicit. Phrase them as COACHING prompts ("is there an established tool/standard for this?", "is this one coherent proven approach or a stitched-together composite?") consistent with this epic's coach-not-roadblock posture.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
Three-pass review standard (to adopt for plan review) — locked from the review-structure realignment in epic 9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b:

1. Pass 1 emits records: {finding, criteria[] (each in the prompt's locked rubric set), evidence[] (flexible free text — code citations, prose, OR absence rationale), scenarios[], impact}. No model-emitted severity/confidence.
2. Pass 2 is a SEPARATE verifier agent (independent context) that re-grounds in the source and emits, per finding: severity attributes {prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility} + typed BINARY verification sub-answers {verdict: yes|no|insufficient, evidence_span}. Rules: atomic, independent (don't show the finding's own conclusion in each sub-question), verdict-with-citation NOT verdict-with-fix, allow "insufficient".
3. Pass 3 is DETERMINISTIC: conditional veto (cited-code-reference-accuracy, ONLY when a code citation is present) + graded fraction of the remaining binary checks -> confidence; severity computed from attributes; block vs advisory decided against per-criteria thresholds (project-set, start high -> mostly advisory). No model holistic severity/confidence in the decision path; no interactive escalation (autonomous).

Plan-review-specific note: plan/non-code reviews often have NON-citable evidence (absence findings like "doesn't implement feature X" have no line to cite), which is exactly why evidence is a flexible field and the cited-reference veto applies ONLY when a code citation exists. Research basis: Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE, RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK ("checklists beat reward models"); binary decomposition + deterministic aggregation is better-calibrated than a single LLM confidence number or high/med/low rating.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
FINALIZE SESSION (criteria v7->v8 + three-pass adoption + non-DSO validation). Full writeup: docs/experiments/plan-review-finalize-and-threepass.md. 

THREE-PASS ADOPTED (per directive; from epic 9da1 + log 9dba): the gate now uses evidence->binary-verify->deterministic-gate, REPLACING per-criterion model-emitted severity/confidence. Pass1 emits findings {finding,criteria[],evidence[],scenarios[],impact} NO severity; Pass2 separate verifier emits severity attributes {prod/debt_impact,blast_radius,likelihood,reversibility} + typed binary sub-answers {yes|no|insufficient} (atomic, independent, verdict-with-citation, allow insufficient); Pass3 deterministic: cited-ref veto (only when a code citation present) -> graded-fraction confidence -> severity-from-attributes -> block|advisory|dropped vs per-criterion thresholds (start high -> advisory-only in v1; only the DET floor blocks). Descriptor set criteria_v8.json (severity_by=pass3, default_posture=advisory, block_threshold, checklist[], applies_at).

REGISTRY HARDENING (v7->v8): (D) checklist[] lifted into all 31 descriptors (binary sub-checks; Q10). (A) applies_at lifted from retune.py into the registry (levels/container_only/suppress_types[bug]/suppress_when[test_task,mechanical_leaf]); E5 retuned (gate on introduces-testable, raise bar, story+task only). (E) parse hardening (gate_lib.robust_findings: never crash on no_tool_use/empty/malformed; synth INDETERMINATE for missing ids; retries) — 126/126 chunk calls ok in E4.

NON-DSO GENERALIZATION (E4, 11 rebar + 8 snap real tickets, the held-out validation round-4 lacked): KEY RESULT — single-turn Pass-1-ONLY OVER-FIRES on a new population (G5 FAIL 87%, E6 100%, E1 80%, E5 64%, T7 67%), and the agentic verification CORRECTS the false-fails (e249 E4 FAIL->PASS, G6/G1G2 AMBIGUOUS->PASS, grounded in real rebar code). => unverified Pass-1 severity is the over-fire source; the three-pass verify+deterministic-aggregate is the fix. Confirms the architectural choice empirically.

OVERLAY-TRIGGER PRECISION (E3): deterministic keyword triggers over-fire on plans -> T10/T11/T12 moved to LLM-ROUTING. T11 'migration' det fired 2/19 (both bash->py strangler, NOT data); T12 det 7/19; LLM router 0 on both (correct). 

DECISIVENESS LEVER A/B: recall 23/24->24/24; G6 clean-case AMBIGUOUS:3->PASS:3 (lever fixes G6 hedge w/o tools); COH/E5/T11/T12/A1 perfect at single-turn (COH needs NO agent tier — kept 1-TURN; corrects the earlier STATUS list).

THREE-PASS CAPSTONE on THIS epic (all 31 criteria, Pass1 Opus, Pass2 agentic vs rebar): DET floor PASS (check-ac 13 lines; clarity 7). Pass1 -> 3 findings; Pass3 -> 1 dropped(conf .29), 2 advisory, 0 block. All 3 converge on T12 rollout/rollback: REAL GAP found = the gate adds a blocking claim-path change but never specifies a kill-switch/disable-flag/rollback for the GATE ITSELF (advisory, conf .71, critical). The deterministic filter correctly DROPPED the one finding the plan already addresses (--force + out-of-band). Recommend filing a follow-on: gate kill-switch / disable-config + rollout-ordering for the signature scheme.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: TIER CORRECTION (agent-vs-single-turn). Code-grounding is the SOLE responsibility of E4/G1G2/A1 (+ G6 for mechanism-correctness); no other criterion independently greps the codebase. Reclassified G3/G4 (parent-vs-child TICKET analysis — fed as context) and T10/T11 (plan-intrinsic IaC/migration safety from ticket + best-practice knowledge; existence/convention/NIH checks defer to E4/G1G2/A1; no .tf/schema in a plan) from AGENT -> single-turn. Remaining AGENT-tier (truly probe code/external/web): G1G2, E4, A1, G6, T8, T1, T3. Same root cause as the intent-source-fidelity mis-tag: 'needs frontier model + large context' was conflated with 'needs the tool-using loop'. The bright line is captured for documentation in remediation epic 6f2d. criteria_v8.json + check_registry_coverage AGENT_TIER + three_pass CODEBASE_GROUNDED updated; guard passes.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PREREQUISITE FOUNDATIONS SHIPPED — epic + children realigned (2026-06).

Three foundations this epic was written to build ON have landed; updated the epic description (new '## Prerequisite foundations — NOW SHIPPED' section + corrected the now-false 'none wired into rebar today' polyglot claim + the spun-out carve-out line) and the affected children to consume them rather than build them. The closed prerequisite epics themselves were NOT touched.

1. Code-grounding evidence oracle — epic 8f6c (CLOSED). src/rebar/grounding/: polyglot, fail-open, three-valued oracle (refute_absence/applies/scan) — T0 deps existence (deps.dev + optional syft), T1 symbol/import/file resolution (universal-ctags), Engine B detectors (OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard), grounding_info static contract. Layer-1 P2/P3/P4 CONSUME it; this epic adds NO new tool integrations. T2 LSP-semantic resolution = follow-on epic 850f (open).

2. LLM workflow engine — epic e6d9 (CLOSED, 'Workflow engine v2'). src/rebar/llm/workflow/: declarative IR + interpreter/executor, run_workflow (CLI + MCP), PydanticAI runner + structured-output stack, Inspect-AI eval seam (src/rebar/llm/eval.py). The gate runs AS a workflow on this engine.

3. Prompt storage / contract-bearing prompt model — epic da27 (all 11 children closed; epic closed THIS session with a signed completion verdict). src/rebar/llm/prompts.py: front-matter closed-key contracts (inputs/outputs schema-names, execution_mode, category), derived prompt index + CI drift gate, .rebar/prompts/ override resolution, canonical writer + editor authoring. The criteria registry's 'reuse rebar.llm catalog/contracts/prompt/runner' is now this concrete machinery.

Child edits: 012e (Layer-1 floor) now consumes the 8f6c oracle facade (was: wrap solidlsp/ast-grep/syft/scc/lizard as new integrations); ca03 (orchestrator) builds on the shipped engine + prompt library; 7284 (eval suite) wires the shipped eval seam.

Prototype harness for validation: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/final_gate_3pass.py against criteria_v8.json (the same harness da27's own plan-review run used).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
ADVISORY FINDINGS ADDRESSED (from the 2026-06 validation harness run).

Triaged the 10 advisory findings; addressed the valid ones (the dropped <0.5-conf findings were not surfaced). 11 epic edits + 4 child edits + 1 link:

- T8 (conf 1.00) PRIORITY-SCORE FORMULA was undefined (block_threshold 0.95 semantically empty): specified the v1 default in the Pass-3 section + child 487d — validity = graded fraction of binary sub-answers ∈[0,1]; impact = deterministic mean of the 5 Pass-2 severity attributes ordinal-mapped to [0,1]; PRIORITY = validity × impact ∈[0,1]; block iff (not vetoed) AND priority ≥ block_threshold; drop if validity<.5. Weights marked calibration-tunable.
- T8 (finding-id): cross-referenced the orchestrator-mints-content-fingerprint-id contract (already owned by ca03; 75a9/db7b already consume it) into the epic priority-CAP bullet.
- E1 (conf .86) P7-vs-P8: added P8 reviewability/context-budget to the canonical Layer-1 registry list + the top-level AC; updated 'DET (P1–P7)' → 'P1–P8' in the execution-tier + set-size notes (children + the DET-exec-path clarification already said P1–P8).
- T12 (conf .86/.57): aligned the prose to criteria_v8 — T12 is 1-TURN LLM-routed (removed the stale 'needs the live graph / agent-tier' overclaim in the AGENT-tier-contract section), and surfaced its deployed-surface APPLIES predicate (PASS not-applicable on libraries/CLIs — its core anti-FP) + the 3 sub-checks (staged_rollout/rollback/deploy_ordering). Added a T12 RECALL fixture AC to the eval child 7284 (validate discrimination, not just suppression).
- G6 (conf .86) Pass-2 mode: clarified 'single aggregate call' = ONE aggregate verification pass over ALL findings (not per-finding), agentic for codebase-grounded + single-turn otherwise — not a no-tools one-shot.
- COH (conf .71) G3/G4 tier: reconciled the registry prose to AGENT-tier container-only (matching criteria_v8 + the later AGENT-tier-contract decision); removed the contradictory 'single-turn ticket-analysis'.
- G4 (conf .86) ISF scope overlap: 7284 no longer 're-builds' the ISF criterion (681b owns the build); 7284 owns the eval suite + ISF eval cases; added a relates_to link 7284↔681b and ownership notes on both.
- T5a (conf .57) container fan-out cost: budget formula now adds a per-CHILD agent-call term for the AGENT-tier G3/G4 container criteria.

NOT actioned: G1G2 verify_completion anchor (conf .57) — near-FP: rebar.llm.verify_completion + src/rebar/llm/completion.py exist and the gate just ran on da27; only the CLI help filename differs.

Re-running the harness to confirm resolution + no regressions.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
VALIDATION RE-RUN + round-2 contradiction fixes (2026-06).

Re-ran final_gate_3pass.py on the updated epic + 13 children after the findings edits. RESULT: DET floor PASS; 24 Pass-1 findings -> 0 BLOCK, 13 advisory, 11 dropped. Confirmed: (a) the 8 findings addressed in the prior pass did NOT recur; (b) NO regression — none of the re-run's findings reference the realignment/findings edits as a new contradiction.

The re-run surfaced a fresh subset of the perennial advisory backlog (the gate is non-deterministic; each agentic run resamples ~12 of the epic's standing advisory findings). Of these, three were clean self-contradictions and got fixed:
- COH/E1 (.86) — 012e AC said 'DET floor is the only tier that blocks' (absolute) vs the epic's opt-in LLM blocking. FIXED: qualified 012e to 'only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1; per-criterion LLM blocking is opt-in via block_threshold'.
- COH/E1 (.57) — Success-criterion #1 (claim 'requires' attestation, unconditional) vs SC#4 (preference DEFAULTS OFF). FIXED: SC#1 now reads 'WHEN THE GATE IS ENABLED (SC#4; default OFF) ... default OFF => claim keeps today's behavior'.
- T8 (1.00) — the Pass-4 SUBJECT VALIDATOR predicate/fallback were unspecified (the load-bearing C1 enforcement). FIXED in 75a9: v1-default predicate (<=8 words/<=60 chars; reject code tokens / leading imperative) + fallback (emit no coaching, best-effort drop).

NOT chased further (by design): the remaining advisory findings (E2/E5/G5 decomposition + eval-labeling; T5b transport-failure handling; E6/F1 latency target; T5a prompt-cache-bust cost across revision rounds; T12 new-event-type cross-clone coexistence; 6c5c docs-gap) are valid IMPLEMENTATION-TIME considerations the v1 advisory gate is designed to SURFACE not block, captured in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar — not contradictions or regressions. Re-running again only resamples the backlog (non-deterministic), so it is not run-to-green.

All edited tickets pass check-ac + clarity (epic clarity 7/5).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
ADVISORY BACKLOG REMEDIATED (2026-06). Rather than leave the remaining valid advisory findings as implementation-time backlog, resolved them in the spec (6 epic + 4 child edits; all gates pass):

- E1 (docs gap): epic Docs AC now includes the honest end-to-end time-to-first-work + Pass-4 coach items that only child 6c5c carried.
- E6/F1 (latency proving): the 50ms fast-claim target's in-session proving check is STRUCTURAL (092b's 'no LLM/network on the claim path' — a pure local HMAC verify), not a wall-clock benchmark; real p50/p95 captured on the sidecar.
- T5a (cache-bust cost): budget clarified — cap is PER REVIEW RUN; the edit-then-re-review convergence loop (~2-3 runs) busts the prefix cache, so cost-to-signature ≈ cap × revisions (no cross-run cache saving assumed), observed via the sidecar.
- G5 (big-bang risk): added a risk-ordered MVP BUILD ORDER — a thin end-to-end vertical slice (DET floor + orchestrator + one single-tur­n criterion + Pass-1/2/3 + attestation + claim) proving the full claim→review→sign→claim loop before fanning out.
- E2 (eval labeling): RESOLVED the open auto-labels-vs-human decision — auto-labels seed the bulk corpus; the GATING gold subset is human-adjudicated (epic AC + 7284).
- E5 (Pass-2 has no proving check): acc1 gains a discrimination proving check (planted true/false findings; Pass-2 must filter the false without rubber-stamping) wired into 7284.
- T5b (claim signing-infra unavailability): 092b — when ENABLED and the signing/verification machinery is unavailable (missing key/subsystem error), claim FAILS CLOSED (consistent with the close gate), --force is the escape; DISABLED → unchanged.
- T12 (cross-clone event coexistence): db7b — REVIEW_RESULT + attestation events follow rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout (fsck WARN; reconcile hosts upgrade first).
- E4 (unverified facade/claim assertions): near-FP — verified refute_absence/applies/scan ARE exported from src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py (now cited in the epic) and the atomic claim primitive exists.

This concludes the plan-review epic work.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness): CLEAN, no findings.

comments.body:
PASS-1 PLAN REVIEW — [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: PASS (no findings).

I assessed the plan's CHOSEN APPROACH against all four G6 dimensions, grounding mechanism-correctness reasoning in the shipped/implemented code (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/{attest,passes,orchestrator}.py) and the closed prerequisite epics (8f6c, e6d9, da27 verified CLOSED with concrete src/rebar/grounding/ + workflow engine + prompt model).

(1) MECHANISM CORRECTNESS — sound. The load-bearing mechanisms hold up, including the edge cases G6 specifically probes:
- TOCTOU / check-then-act (the exact hazard in the G6 checklist): EXPLICITLY raised and resolved in "Design clarifications — CLAIM ATOMICITY (T9)" — the fast signature-verify + open→in_progress are ONE atomic step reusing rebar's existing atomic claim primitive (re-read under lock + optimistic concurrency / ConcurrencyError), NOT a check-then-act sequence. Grounded: attest.claim_gate_check is a pure local HMAC verify + light fingerprint recompute with no LLM/network, fail-closed-when-enabled (unverifiable-material → ok:False), so the atomic claim primitive owns the race.
- Material-edit invalidation data-flow is complete: signature binds the material fingerprint; current_material_fingerprint recomputes from a light read; stale-head AND stale-material both gate; read failure → fail-closed (never wrongly passes).
- Convergence-loop cost mechanism (cap × revisions, prefix-bust each round) is internally consistent and honestly caveated (no cross-run cache saving assumed; per-run cost captured on the sidecar to OBSERVE not benchmark).
- Cap never drops BLOCKING findings (block decision can't be weakened by the advisory cap) — verified in orchestrator (DEFAULT_ADVISORY_CAP, blocking EXEMPT). No hidden ordering/atomicity assumption found.

(2) FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — solves the named problem (unsound plans executed at open→in_progress), and notably AVOIDS the proxy trap: the cert means "process followed, no red flags + coverage", NOT "perfect" (the round-5 "fail-open hides a hollow signal" critique was examined and resolved with coverage-recorded + sidecar verdicts + client-composed CI predicate).

(3) APPROACH SELECTION — I generated two structurally-different alternatives and judged the chosen approach defensible:
  • (A) inline-blocking-at-claim — the plan weighs this (option A) and rejects it on latency + autonomous-agent confabulation + compound-error grounds; defensibly worse on latency/blast-radius.
  • (B) cache the verdict in a non-signed material-hash sidecar (drop HMAC) — structurally different at the data/signing layer, but loses the composable CI rigor signal (a NAMED requirement) and forgery/replay resistance.
Both are defensibly NOT superior → discarded, not written into the plan (per the anti-priming rule). The plan correctly carries exactly ONE approach (option C, decoupled signed attestation) while documenting the A/B/C weighing in "Design decisions revisited (round 5)".

(4) POSITIVE RATIONALE — present and abundant (why decoupled out-of-band; why advisory-Layer-2 over block-by-default — >30% FP → tool ignored; why deterministic Pass-3; why validity×impact as ONE score; why dep-graph-need ⇒ agent-tier). No rejected-alternatives section is forced (correctly anti-priming).

ANTI-FP honored: this is a genuine design with real choices (not a forced/mechanical change), so alternative-generation was warranted; correctness reasoning was grounded in the actual code via tools, not manufactured.

affirmations: G6 — mechanism correctness (incl. the named TOCTOU/atomicity hazard, explicitly resolved); fitness-for-purpose (solves the problem, not a proxy); approach selection defensible vs reviewer-generated alternatives (no clearly-superior missed option); positive rationale stated for the chosen approach.

  - suggested_fix: Add a one-line note where the moves are enumerated, e.g. "(id 10 reserved/removed — intentionally unused)", so the enum is unambiguous for both the executor and project extenders.

- [ ] Reconcile the MOVE REGISTRY descriptor SCHEMA with the conditional-firing rules the plan references, and co-locate the `has_children`/per-review gating with the schema that carries it. The plan describes the moves only as "a fixed, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY" the Pass-4 call "maps each finding to a move from", but it ALSO asserts conditional firing — "11 propagate-revisions-to-children" and (in child 75a9 §What, which this epic's design summary points to) "11 … (fires when the ticket has children)", "12 … (fires per review)". The epic never declares a descriptor slot for those triggers, nor where the has-children guard is applied. Tool-grounded: the shipped registry is `{move_id: {name, template}}` only — no `triggers`/`source` field — and the Pass-4 selector is the LLM emitting a `move_id` with no `has_children` signal threaded in, so move-11 ("Propagate the revision for {subject} to the child tickets") can be selected on a CHILDLESS ticket. T8(b): a decision rule (conditional move-firing) referenced but not co-located with the schema/step that needs it (the `has_children` context).
  - criteria: T8
  - location: Design summary §Pass-4 affirmative COACH ("maps each finding to a move from a fixed, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY"; "11 propagate-revisions-to-children"); §Criteria registry (registry-descriptor pattern); cf. child 75a9 §What ("each move = {id, triggers (criteria/finding-class), template, source}", "fires when the ticket has children")
  - evidence: the epic asserts conditional move-firing but specifies no registry slot or processing step for it; tool-confirmed orchestrator.MOVE_REGISTRY entries are {name, template} only (no triggers/source) and pass4_coach receives only the surviving findings + registry, never has_children — so the move-11 trigger guard is unenforced by anything the plan names.
  - suggested_fix: In the Pass-4 section, either (a) declare the move-descriptor schema the epic intends (e.g. {id, triggers, template, source}) and state WHERE the has-children / per-review gate is applied (deterministically, before or after LLM selection), or (b) drop the conditional-firing language and state that move 11 is only offered when the orchestrator passes a has-children flag — so the executor knows the trigger semantics are real and where they live.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes):
- (a) The Pass-4 SUBJECT VALIDATOR enum/value-vocabulary is fully defined where it is referenced: bounded noun-phrase, code-token/imperative rejection list — the one free-text field the locked template interpolates is fully bounded.
- (b) Decision-rule co-located with its schema for the in-decision-path passes: the Pass-3 deterministic decision math (validity = graded fraction; impact = ordinal→[0,1] mean; priority = validity×impact; block iff priority ≥ block_threshold) sits beside the Pass-2 attribute + binary-sub-answer enums it consumes; the SUBJECT VALIDATOR + deterministic-render rule sit beside the move-registry schema.
- (c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: cap/block ownership is explicit and disambiguated — Pass-3/the orchestrator (ca03) owns the top-N advisory cap + the block decision; "the cap NEVER drops BLOCKING findings"; the finding `id` is minted by exactly one owner (ca03) and consumed by Pass-4/the sidecar.
- (d) A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step IS specified throughout: fail-open abstain→PASS on unsupported-stack/tool-crash/timeout; INDETERMINATE on budget cap-hit (sheds AGENT/overlay first); Pass-4 best-effort drop (rejected subject / no matching move ⇒ no coaching, never a retry-into-prose loop); Pass-2 `insufficient` verdict allowed.
- (e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern in the LLM surface: the Pass-4 "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored deterministic template (the LLM authors no prose), enforced mechanically by the subject validator rather than via a load-bearing negative instruction; referenced agents/contracts (ca03 id-minter, db7b sidecar, 9da1 shared framework, the 8f6c grounding oracle facade) exist and reciprocally specify their contracts.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection).

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this epic satisfies):
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the chosen architecture (decoupled signed-attestation "Option C": fast HMAC verify on claim + heavy review out-of-band) solves the NAMED problem (an open→in_progress gate that doesn't roadblock autonomous agents) rather than a proxy. The CLAIM ATOMICITY clarification (T9) correctly grounds the verify-and-transition in rebar's existing atomic claim primitive (re-read under lock + optimistic concurrency), foreclosing the TOCTOU check-then-act race G6 specifically probes for.
- APPROACH SELECTION + POSITIVE RATIONALE: the plan explicitly weighed structurally-different alternatives — A blocking-inline / B advisory / C decoupled-attestation — and chose C with a stated positive rationale (the "Design decisions revisited (round 5)" section + the brainstorm narrative); block-by-default-with-routine-override was explicitly rejected on an FP-ceiling argument. This is the rejected-alternatives reasoning done in the design record, NOT primed into the implementer's plan. Strong.
- The three-pass verdict model's central mechanism — model emits NO holistic severity/confidence; a separate verifier emits atomic binary sub-answers + ordinal attributes; a DETERMINISTIC Pass-3 aggregates — is mechanism-sound and research-grounded (CoVe/FactScore/TICK), and is verified against the canonical harness (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/three_pass.py pass3_decide).
- AGENT-tier tool-access + dep-graph-need⇒agent-tier routing rule: a sound mechanism-correctness fix grounded in a real dry-run FP (partial-view snapshot yields false "missing sequencing edge"); reclassifying G3/G4/COH to read the live graph is the correct data-flow choice.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Reconcile the "unified priority score" mechanism between the Design-summary narrative and the canonical Pass-3 implementation — the block decision's data-flow is described two incompatible ways. The narrative repeatedly states the priority score (validity × impact) is "the ONE number driving BOTH the block threshold and the advisory ranking" and "not two separate gates" (Design summary §Unified priority score; Pass-3 §"UNIFIED PRIORITY SCORE = validity × impact … the ONE number driving both the block threshold and the advisory ranking"; AC "Unified priority score … drives the block threshold AND advisory ranking"). But the canonical harness blocks on TWO separate gates — `confidence >= block_threshold AND severity in ("major","critical")` (three_pass.py:313) — NOT on the product `priority >= block_threshold`. The Pass-3 prose itself also says "BLOCK iff … priority ≥ the criterion's block_threshold (default 0.95)", which contradicts both the harness AND the same paragraph's earlier "validity × impact ∈ [0,1]" (a 0.95 product threshold is near-unreachable since it needs validity≈1 AND impact≈1, whereas the harness's two-gate form is satisfiable). An implementer cannot tell whether the block predicate is (a) priority-product ≥ threshold, or (b) confidence ≥ threshold AND severity-bucket high. Pick ONE block predicate and make the narrative, the Pass-3 section, and the descriptor `block_threshold` semantics agree.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan Design summary §"Unified priority score + advisory surfacing CAP"; §"Review structure — three-pass" Pass-3; AC "Unified priority score …"; vs docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/three_pass.py:308-314
  - evidence: plan: "ONE priority score (not two separate gates)" and "BLOCK iff … priority ≥ the criterion's block_threshold (default 0.95)"; harness: `elif blocking_enabled and confidence >= block_threshold and severity in ("major","critical")` — a two-gate predicate, not the product; a product ≥ 0.95 has very different reachability than the two-gate form.

- [ ] State the POSITIVE rationale for the load-bearing magic constants in the Pass-3→[0,1] aggregation, OR mark them explicitly as the calibration-tunable v1 default shape at the point of definition. The plan asserts the priority score is "validity × impact" but the canonical severity math is a different, unexplained shape: `score = impact + 0.5*(blast-1) + 0.5*(like-1) + 0.34*(rev-1)` bucketed at ≥4.0/≥2.5 (three_pass.py:308-309), while the plan's prose describes impact as "the deterministic MEAN of the Pass-2 severity attributes" — a mean and that weighted-sum-plus-buckets are not the same function, and the 0.5/0.34/4.0/2.5 weights have no stated rationale. G6(4) requires a positive rationale for the chosen approach; the product/mean/weight choices are the mechanism that converts attributes into the block decision, so their derivation (or an explicit "v1 default, weights calibration-tunable — see threshold-calibration carve-out") should be stated where the math is defined, not left as bare constants that disagree between prose and harness.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §"Review structure" Pass-3 (impact = "deterministic mean of the Pass-2 severity attributes"); vs docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/three_pass.py:302-309
  - evidence: plan: "IMPACT ∈ [0,1] = the deterministic mean of the Pass-2 severity attributes"; harness uses a weighted SUM (`impact + 0.5*(blast-1) + …`) with bucketing, not a mean; weights 0.5/0.34 and cut-points 4.0/2.5 carry no positive rationale in the plan.

- [ ] Close the mechanism gap between the iteration-fatigue failure mode the plan names and the mitigation it ships. The plan correctly identifies that a fatigued reviser "tends to try to FORCE PAST a blocked gate" (§Iteration-fatigue) and offers `--force`+justification as the escape hatch — but the ONLY named mitigations (the advisory cap + never-drop-blocking) address ADVISORY VOLUME, not the force-past behavior, and `--force` is itself the unbounded lever the named failure mode would exploit (a fatigued agent force-claims past any block). Either (a) state why `--force` does not re-open the very failure mode (e.g. the override is logged to REVIEW_RESULT and the convergence loop is bounded, so a forced claim is observable/auditable, not silent), or (b) acknowledge that mitigating advisory volume does not constrain force-past and route that residual to the offline FP/override analysis as an explicit non-goal for v1. As written the cap is offered as the mitigation for a failure mode it does not actually constrain.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §"Iteration-fatigue failure mode (why the cap + never-drop-blocking matter)"; SC#1 / AC "claim integration: … --force … override"
  - evidence: plan: "the model tends to try to FORCE PAST a blocked gate rather than keep addressing feedback. A bounded, prioritized, convergent advisory surface mitigates this" — but the advisory cap governs non-blocking volume, whereas the force-past behavior targets the BLOCK; the `--force` override is the mechanism a fatigued agent would use, and the plan does not connect it to the fatigue mitigation.

FINDINGS

- [ ] The plan's load-bearing premise — that the gate "never specifies how to **disable/roll back the gate itself**" and is "a gate with no off-switch [== a] single point of failure" — is **refuted by the current codebase and shipped docs**; verify this premise before building, since AC1 (project-level disable config, fail-open) appears to already exist. Evidence: `rebar.toml:14-21` documents `require_plan_review_for_claim` with the inline note "Turning this off is the rollback"; `docs/plan-review-gate.md:178-197` states the flag defaults to `false`, "**Turning it off is the rollback** — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed", and "Default **off** ⇒ `claim` keeps today's behavior exactly." That is a project-level, fail-open disable switch identical to what Scope bullet 1 / AC1 propose to add.
  - criteria: E4
  - location: Why ("A gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure"), Scope bullet 1, AC line 1 ("A config flag disables the plan-review gate so `claim` is unchanged (fail-open) when off")

- [ ] AC1 also asks for a test that "covers" the gate-off/fail-open behavior; verify whether `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py::test_gate_off_by_default_claims_without_attestation` (line 69; plus `test_plan_review_gate.py:230-239` exercising `load_config(...).require_plan_review_for_claim` True/False) already satisfies it, so the AC does not re-specify existing coverage.

- [ ] The premise that `--force` is "not a system-level kill switch" is correct AS STATED, but reconcile it with the existing config flag: `--force` is the per-claim escape (`tests/golden/cli_help/claim__dashhelp.out`: "--force bypasses the plan-review gate"; `docs/plan-review-gate.md:210`), while `require_plan_review_for_claim=false` IS the system-level off-switch. The plan's Why frames the absence of a system-level switch as the gap, which the config flag already fills.
  - criteria: E4
  - location: Why ("The gate's coaching posture + `--force` per-claim is not a system-level kill switch")

VERIFIED ASSERTIONS (premise checks that PASS — no finding)

- "blocking change to the in-use `claim` (open->in_progress) path": VERIFIED via Read/Grep — `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` `_plan_review_precheck` wired into the claim path (per `test_plan_review_gate.py:3`), and gate-ON-blocks-claim is tested (`test_plan_review_gate.py:76-84`).

NOTE: The plan cites a verifier advisory (conf .71, critical) and `final_gate_cycle3.json:1182` ("rollback... reverts the GATE BEHAVIOR but not the persisted event-log artifacts") — the *genuinely* unaddressed slice of T12 is the **persisted REVIEW_RESULT/SIGNATURE events after a binary downgrade** (deploy-ordering/coexistence), NOT the gate on/off switch. The behavioral-disable half of Scope/AC1 looks already-shipped; consider rescoping the plan to the artifact-rollback/deploy-ordering half (AC2) and dropping or reframing AC1 around the existing flag + test.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 PLAN REVIEW — [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — on the kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering FOLLOW-ON plan ("T12 gap the gate found on itself").

I grounded every load-bearing premise of this plan in the shipped code (no reliance on the plan's own framing). The central conclusion: the approach is NOT fit-for-purpose because the mechanism it proposes to build ALREADY EXISTS AND SHIPS — making this a NIH/rebuild-existing approach defect rather than a sound design. Findings below are coaching, non-blocking.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Re-ground the plan's central premise — "the gate introduces a **blocking change to the in-use `claim` path** but never specifies how to **disable/roll back the gate itself**" — against the shipped code, which contradicts it: the claim gate is an OPT-IN preference that DEFAULTS OFF, so it introduces no blocking change to the in-use claim path until a project enables it, and turning it off IS the rollback. Re-scope the ticket from "design+build a disable-config and rollback path" to "verify the existing default-off preference satisfies these ACs (and fill any genuine doc/test gap), else close as already-satisfied."
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "Why" + "Scope" item 1 ("A project-level disable-the-gate config (gate off => claim behaves exactly as today; fail-open)") + AC line 1
  - evidence:
    - `src/rebar/config.py:260` — `require_plan_review_for_claim: bool = False` (DEFAULT OFF); the inline comment at :258-259 reads "Default off ⇒ `claim` keeps today's behavior; turning it off is the rollback (an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed)."
    - `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:651,661-662` — `_plan_review_precheck` returns `None` (allow) when the preference is off; so when off, `claim` is unchanged.
    - The parent epic's OWN Success Criteria #4 already states this exact design: "a config PREFERENCE that turns the plan-review requirement ON/OFF ... DEFAULTS OFF ... 'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed."
  - suggested_fix: Rewrite "Why"/"Scope" to acknowledge `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default False) IS the disable-the-gate config and IS the rollback; reframe AC1 from "A config flag disables the gate ... covered by a test" to "confirm the existing default-off preference + its test coverage satisfy this" (see the test note below).

- [ ] AC1's "covered by a test" clause appears already met — verify before treating it as new work. `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py:233` asserts `require_plan_review_for_claim is False` by default and :238-239 asserts the on-toggle; the gate-off path leaving `claim` at today's behavior is also an explicit edge case in the parent epic's end-to-end AC. Point AC1 at the existing test (or name the precise missing assertion) rather than implying a new test must be authored.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan AC line 1 ("... fail-open ... when off; covered by a test")
  - evidence: `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py:44,233-239` (default-off + on-toggle config behavior); parent epic end-to-end AC includes "the gate-off config preference leaving `claim` at today's behavior."

- [ ] Reconcile Scope item 2 (old+new coexistence; "older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events") and Scope item 3 (deploy ordering for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events) with the SHIPPED rollout handling — both are already implemented and were addressed by child db7b, so this is fitness-for-purpose duplication, not a gap. State what (if anything) the shipped handling is missing, or fold these scope items into the re-scoped verification.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "Scope" items 2 and 3 + AC line 2
  - evidence:
    - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:14` — REVIEW_RESULT + attestation events "follow rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout (upgrade reconcile hosts first)" — i.e. both coexistence AND deploy-ordering are stated at the implementation.
    - `docs/event-schema.md:77` + `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py` — unknown event types are preserved-and-ignored, with forward-compat tests; `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:154` handles it in fsck.
    - Parent epic comment (1782271126…): "T12 (cross-clone event coexistence): db7b — REVIEW_RESULT + attestation events follow rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout (fsck WARN; reconcile hosts upgrade first)."
  - suggested_fix: Drop Scope 2/3 as net-new design and instead cross-reference `sidecar.py` + `event-schema.md` + the db7b rollout note as the existing coexistence/ordering design; if a real gap remains (e.g. the SIGNATURE head-state event vs. an old reducer), name it specifically.

- [ ] State the POSITIVE rationale tied to the ACTUAL state of the code, because as written the plan's rationale ("a gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure") rests on a premise that is false against the shipped default-off, opt-in gate. If after re-grounding a genuine residual gap survives (e.g. signing-scheme deploy ordering not yet documented in a project-facing rollback runbook), state why THAT specific gap warrants a ticket; if none survives, the positive rationale is "verify + document," which should be stated explicitly so an implementer does not build a redundant disable mechanism.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "Why" section (whole)
  - evidence: the "Why" asserts the gate "never specifies how to disable/roll back" and "a gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure" — contradicted by `config.py:260` (default off) + `transition.py:661-662` (off ⇒ claim unchanged) + the documented "turning it off is the rollback" (`docs/plan-review-gate.md:180`, `config.py:258-259`).

APPROACH-SELECTION NOTE (G6.3): I generated the structurally-different alternative the plan missed — "treat this as a verification/doc-audit task against the already-shipped default-off preference + preserved-and-ignored rollout, not a build task." On codebase-alignment (the mechanism exists), blast-radius (zero new code vs. a redundant second disable path), simplicity, and robustness (avoids two competing kill-switch concepts), that alternative is clearly superior to "build a new disable-config + rollback path." ## Completion verification result: FAIL

Verified the implementation of the plan-review gate epic against its stated Acceptance Criteria. Most of the epic is well-implemented — the Layer-1 DET floor (P1–P8), Layer-2 advisory criteria via the registry, the three-pass verdict model, attestation/signing, claim integration, REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, Pass-4 coach, eval suite, and docs are all present and substantive. However, several ACs are not met.

### Failing requirements

**AC "Configurable chunking; proportionate-depth routing (complexity + has_children); escalation by size+complexity+centrality (centrality/blast-radius computed at plan time); per-plan budget cap with chunk-atomic checkpointing and an INDETERMINATE outcome (non-blocking default, configurable)"**

`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` implements the size-handling ladder (batch → one-criterion-per-call → model escalation → P8 failure finding) and the advisory cap, but does **not** implement: (a) a per-plan budget cap that tracks spend and sheds agent/overlay criteria first when exceeded, (b) chunk-atomic checkpointing, or (c) centrality/blast-radius escalation computed at plan time. The `INDETERMINATE` verdict exists in the code (line 587) for when Pass-3 receives no verifications — but the "cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first → INDETERMINATE" mechanism is not present. No `budget_cap`, `checkpoint`, or `centrality` logic appears anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`.

**AC "Tests + verification: every AC maps to a concrete proving check; ... cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first) ... the reducer-ignored REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (reducer skips the type; head/.cache/deps/validate unaffected)."**

The cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding test is entirely absent from both `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py` and `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`. Searching `tests/` for "INDETERMINATE", "cap_hit", "cap.hit", "shed.*agent", "indeterminate.*cap" returns no matches. The "fail-open per tool (unsupported-lang / no-server / crash / timeout → skipped=pass)" requirement is partially met (crash → abstain and no-repo tests exist), but there are no specific tests for unsupported-lang, no-server, or timeout scenarios at the P2/P3 oracle layer as stated.

**AC "End-to-end proving check: ... AND its EDGE cases (--force override + logging; material-edit invalidation; fail-open on an unsupported stack; cap-hit INDETERMINATE; the gate-off config preference leaving `claim` at today's behavior)."**

Two of the five required edge cases are not covered in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`: (1) fail-open on an unsupported stack (no test), and (2) cap-hit INDETERMINATE (no test). The other three (--force, material-edit, gate-off) are tested.

### What IS met

- Layer-1 DET floor (P1–P8): implemented polyglot, fail-open, with the P2/P3 oracle facade consumption — `det_floor.py`
- Layer-2 advisory criteria (all F/E/G/A/T overlays + ISF + COH/BROAD): implemented via registry + prompt library — `registry.py`, `passes.py`

- Defaults grounded in experiments — `criteria_routing.json`

**Layer-1 DET floor (P1–P8)** — `det_floor.py`: P1 readiness shape, P2 file/symbol resolution (8f6c oracle), P3 package existence (8f6c oracle), P4 oversize signals, P5 task-DAG validity + cycle detection, P6 AC/DD quality, P7 destructive sniff, P8 reviewability/context-budget. Polyglot, fail-open (abstain on unsupported/crash/timeout), blocking only on P1/P5-cycle/P8.

**Layer-2 advisory criteria** — `registry.py` + `passes.py` + `criteria_routing.json`: all F/E/G/A criteria + triggered T1–T12 overlays + COH + ISF implemented as contract-bearing prompts in the prompt library. Proportionate scrutiny via `applies_at`. 32 criteria in the canonical set.

**Three-pass verdict model** — `passes.py`: Pass-1 emits `{finding, criteria[], evidence[], scenarios[], impact}` with no severity/confidence; Pass-2 separate verifier emits severity attributes + typed binary sub-answers `{yes|no|insufficient}`; Pass-3 deterministic decision (validity = graded fraction, impact = ordinal-mapped mean, priority = validity × impact, block|advisory|dropped).

**Budget cap, checkpointing, centrality** — `sizing.py` + `orchestrator.py`: per-plan budget cap (`REBAR_PLAN_REVIEW_BUDGET`, configurable), `shed_to_budget` sheds lowest-priority AGENT/overlay criteria first → INDETERMINATE, chunk-atomic checkpointing (`load_checkpoint`/`save_checkpoint`, keyed by material fingerprint), centrality/blast-radius computed at plan time from ticket graph.

**Pass-4 coach** — `orchestrator.py` + `passes.py`: move registry (1–9, 11, 12; id 10 intentionally absent/reserved noted), locked templates, `_validate_subject` C1 enforcement (≤8 words/≤60 chars; rejects code/imperatives), project-extensible via `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`.

**Advisory cap** — `orchestrator.py`: top-N by priority (default 10), overflow → sidecar; blocking findings EXEMPT from cap.

**Attestation** — `attest.py`: HMAC-SHA256 signing reusing close-gate machinery, material fingerprint binding, invalidation on material edit, coverage record. Fast claim-gate check (`claim_gate_check`): pure local HMAC verify + fingerprint recompute, no LLM/network.

**Claim integration** — `transition.py` + `_plan_review_precheck`: fast signed-attestation check, `--force`+justification bypass logged as `FORCE_CLAIM` comment, bugs/session_logs exempt.

**REVIEW_RESULT sidecar** — `sidecar.py`: reducer-ignored (`_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`), fsck-recognised, per-finding fingerprints + decisions + verification attributes + metrics, retention-bounded (`prune`).

**Config** — `config.py`: `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` defaults `False` ("turning it off is the rollback").

**Tests** — `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py` + `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`: gate-off default, gate-on blocks, review→claim loop, --force override+logging, material-edit invalidation, bug exemption, DET-floor block→no-signature, REVIEW_RESULT reducer-ignored, no-LLM on claim path (structural), sidecar prune, fail-open on unavailable LLM (`test_review_fail_open_on_unavailable_llm`), cap-hit INDETERMINATE budget shedding (`test_review_cap_hit_indeterminate`), per-tool fail-open for crash/timeout/no-server/unsupported-lang (`test_p2_p3_fail_open_per_tool` parametrized), Pass-3 deterministic math, registry coverage guard, subject validator, checkpointing, centrality.

**Eval suite** — `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`: plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-isf-finder eval specs wired into the Inspect-AI eval seam; recall + false-fire + discrimination pairs from DSO/snap/rebar corpuses; human-adjudicated gold_set; T12 discrimination pair.

**Docs** — `docs/plan-review-gate.md`: gate operation, config surface, fail-open behavior, cert semantics, CI-check pattern, honest end-to-end time (including review latency), Pass-4 coach, Pass-3 math table.

Note: The prior completion-verification comment in this ticket reported several gaps (missing budget-cap/checkpointing/centrality in orchestrator, missing cap-hit INDETERMINATE test, missing fail-open-on-unsupported-stack test). All of these have since been addressed in the current repository — `sizing.py` implements the budget/checkpoint/centrality cluster and is wired into the orchestrator, and both tests exist at `test_plan_review_gate.py:227` and `:246`.

compiled_state.description:
# Plan-review verification gate (open→in_progress)

## Context

rebar has a completion-verifier *close* gate (verifies a ticket's work was actually done before close). It has
no equivalent at the *start* of work: when an agent claims a ticket (open→in_progress), nothing checks that the
ticket's implementation plan is sound before the agent executes it. Flawed plans (too big to one-shot, ambiguous,
wrong file targets, ignored consumers, missing tests, criteria that don't match the work, children that don't
cover the parent, unvalidated novel architecture) get executed anyway, and early errors compound over autonomous
agent trajectories (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects).

This epic builds a **plan-review verification gate** that fires at open→in_progress — the inverse of the
completion-verifier. It is a rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages, frameworks,
project structures, and ticket-creation styles), so it must be polyglot, fail-open on unsupported stacks, and
flexible about inconsistently-scoped tickets. Its posture is to **COACH** agents to author better plans via
positive, well-formed guidance — not to nitpick or roadblock — while emitting a signed **certification** that a
review process was followed (a composable "rigorous agentic development vs vibe-coding" signal for CI).

Full design detail lives in session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` (plan-of-record v4 + the ~30 research reports +
the discussion narrative). This description is the self-contained epic summary.

## Prerequisite foundations — NOW SHIPPED (2026-06)

Three prerequisites this epic was written to build ON have since landed. The design below is
UNCHANGED, but its supporting machinery is now CONCRETE, not aspirational — where the text says
'build/add X', read 'consume the shipped X'. (We are not reopening the closed prerequisite epics;
this section + the per-child notes realign THIS epic to what they delivered.)

- **Code-grounding evidence oracle — epic `8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82` (CLOSED).** `src/rebar/grounding/`
  ships a polyglot, fail-open, THREE-VALUED evidence oracle behind the `refute_absence` / `applies` /
  `scan` facade (exported verbatim from `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py`): T0 dependency existence (deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM), T1 symbol/import/
  file resolution (universal-ctags, 164 langs, no server), Engine B detectors (OpenGrep/ast-grep +
  scc/lizard metrics), plus the static `grounding_info` contract (closed dimension-id / reference-kind
  / abstain-reason vocab + detected backend versions). Layer-1 (P2/P3/P4) CONSUMES this oracle — it no
  longer adds solidlsp/ast-grep/syft/scc/lizard as new integrations. The T2 LSP-semantic-resolution
  follow-on is epic `850f-bf87-8600-4af6` (open), not required for v1.
- **LLM workflow engine — epic `e6d9-41da-da0d-44ef` (CLOSED, 'Workflow engine v2').**
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/` ships the declarative IR + interpreter/executor, `run_workflow` (CLI +
  write-gated MCP tool, mirroring `verify_completion`), the PydanticAI runner + the structured-output
  reliability stack, and the Inspect-AI eval seam (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`). The gate runs AS a
  workflow on this engine; the standing-eval-suite AC wires into the shipped eval seam.
- **Prompt storage / contract-bearing prompt model — epic `da27-c916-f04e-4885` (all children CLOSED;
  epic closed).** `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` ships the front-matter CLOSED-key contract model
  (`inputs`/`outputs` as schema-registry NAMES, `execution_mode` {single_turn|agentic}, `category`),
  the derived prompt index + CI drift gate, `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override resolution (project >
  built-in), and the canonical front-matter writer + in-editor authoring/write-back. The criteria
  registry's 'reuse rebar.llm catalog/contracts/prompt/runner' is now exactly this machinery: each
  criterion ships as a contract-bearing prompt declaring its exec tier; production prompts live in the
  prompt library, not the experiment `criteria_v8.json`.

Per-child realignment: Layer-1 `012e` consumes the oracle facade; orchestrator `ca03` builds on the
shipped engine + prompt library; eval-suite `7284` wires the shipped Inspect-AI eval seam.

NOTE on `criteria_v8.json`: it remains the canonical DESIGN descriptor set + the input to the prototype
harness (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/final_gate_3pass.py`); PRODUCTION criteria graduate
into the shipped prompt library as contract-bearing prompts.

## Design summary

- **Trigger / enforcement:** fires at open→in_progress. `claim` is a FAST signature-verify check only (no LLM, no
  network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms). The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND via a dedicated entrypoint
  (`rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`), triggered by
  the agent on a claim-block or by CI. A REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the client revises and re-runs to earn a
  fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). Routing pivots on **has_children + complexity + ticket LEVEL/TYPE** (proportionate scrutiny — see below), NOT ticket type alone.
- **Two layers:** LAYER 1 = a minimal, deterministic, structural floor that BLOCKS (frozen, unambiguous, cheap,
  polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open; convergent by construction). LAYER 2 = an ADVISORY coaching review
  (per-criterion judgment + one bounded broad open-ended pass) that SURFACES findings authored for remediation
  and never blocks by default.
- **Verdict model — THREE-PASS (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate; see the dedicated section
  below):** the model does NOT emit severity or confidence. Pass 1 surfaces grounded FINDINGS; a SEPARATE Pass-2
  verifier emits severity attributes + atomic binary sub-answers; a DETERMINISTIC Pass 3 computes confidence +
  severity and decides `block | advisory | dropped`. A review-level INDETERMINATE remains for budget/tool-unavailable.
  Defaults ship advisory in v1, but blocking is NOT DET-only: the DET floor blocks structurally AND each
  LLM-tier criterion can block when a project opts it in / sets its `block_threshold` (we ship thresholds
  HIGH so almost everything is advisory during calibration). Per-criterion blocking + a `--force`-override
  are project-configurable. This REPLACES the earlier per-criterion PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL+model-severity model (adopted from
  the shared three-pass framework, epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b`).
- **Coaching / feedback spec:** the reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent can't self-detect
  (research: LLMs can't FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once located). Each advisory finding is grounded
  in a criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked + capped, affirms what already passes, a
  suggested fix only when confident, expressed as a checklist the agent addresses.
- **Pass-4 affirmative COACH (advisory; single-turn, no tooling):** a fourth pass AFTER Pass-3, over only the
  SURVIVING (advisory, not-dropped) findings, priming the caller with a productive NEXT MOVE per finding — beyond
  returning findings (the gate's T2 can flag 'should spike' as a *finding*, but coaching turns surviving findings
  into forward MOVES). It is a SINGLE-TURN STRUCTURED-OUTPUT call (NOT an agent): it maps each finding to a move
  from a fixed, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY and extracts a short subject — output `{move_id, subject,
  finding_refs[]}`, clustered by move — and the coaching prose is rendered DETERMINISTICALLY from the move's
  locked template (the LLM never authors free prose, so it cannot reflect-prompt, hand a solution, restate
  findings, or flatter). LLM-recipient guardrails (positive-by-construction): C1 each note is actionable GUIDANCE
  — a move to pursue (run a spike, research prior art, weigh an alternative), NOT executable code/commands or the
  solution (enforced by a SUBJECT VALIDATOR — `subject` is a bounded noun-phrase, code/imperatives rejected — so
  C1 holds mechanically, not merely via the template); C2 reference findings BY ID (caller-visible finding `id`, no restatement); C3 select-from-registry +
  fill-subject (the template enforces name-the-move-not-the-answer); C4 cap/cluster/front-load. Moves: 1 spike ·
  2 prior-art/OSS-research · 3 pre-mortem · 4 riskiest-assumption-test · 5 weigh-alternatives · 6 specification-
  by-example · 7 thin-vertical-slice · 8 ADR/one-way-door · 9 plan-the-verification · 11 propagate-revisions-to-
  children · 12 generalize-the-finding-across-the-work. Grounded in LLM-feedback research (external-grounded >
  self-reflection; anti-sycophancy; lost-in-the-middle). Coaching is ADVISORY (never blocks) and applies equally
  to the `9da1` code reviewers — see the Pass-4 coach child.
- **Unified priority score + advisory surfacing CAP (config-driven; default 10):** validity and impact are BOTH
  LLM-judged spectrums (neither absolute), so Pass-3 combines them DETERMINISTICALLY into ONE priority score (not
  two separate gates) — used for both the block threshold and the advisory ranking (a low-validity/high-impact
  finding can outrank a high-validity/low-impact one). The surfaced ADVISORY (non-blocking) findings are capped at
  the top-N by that score, N = a CONFIG value (default **10**, owned by `55de`); advisory overflow beyond N -> the
  `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar, not surfaced (volume is the lever that preserves an LLM's action — the cap, not the
  label). **The cap NEVER drops BLOCKING findings — blocking is EXEMPT; if blocking findings exceed N, ALL of them
  are returned** (the cap can never weaken the block decision). Default 10 is grounded by a cap-dilution experiment
  (high-value remediation held ~0.99->0.91 across surfaced lists of 4-32 for a strong single-pass reviser -> 10 is
  well inside the no-degradation zone). EXPERIMENT LIMITATIONS — valuable but INCONCLUSIVE ALONE: it did NOT test a
  smaller model (e.g. Sonnet) and did NOT run under CONTEXT PRESSURE (a common trigger for agents shortcutting /
  ignoring feedback); 10 is a config DEFAULT to TUNE from real-world dogfood data, not a validated constant. Each surfaced finding carries a stable `id` — a content fingerprint MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`) when it
  assembles the post-Pass-3 result, its SOLE owner; the Pass-4 coach (`75a9`) and the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar
  (`db7b`) reference findings BY this id (never restating them).
- **Iteration-fatigue failure mode (why the cap + never-drop-blocking matter):** LLMs get frustrated across review
  rounds — if the 3rd/4th round keeps surfacing NEW findings, the model tends to try to FORCE PAST a blocked gate
  rather than keep addressing feedback. A bounded, prioritized, convergent advisory surface mitigates this; and
  because blocking findings are never dropped by the cap, the block decision is never weakened to placate a
  fatigued reviser.
- **Criteria registry:** each criterion is a declarative descriptor carrying `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP |
  AGENT) + `facet` (the concern/context it examines) + `context_needs` + `routing` + `trigger`; a generic
  chunker reads these (reuse rebar.llm's catalog/contracts/prompt/runner) to bucket by tier and pack same-facet
  criteria into chunks — no hand-maintained chunk lists. Frozen within a review loop (convergence), grown between versions.
- **Polyglot tooling (fail-open IRONCLAD) — CONSUMES the shipped code-grounding oracle (prereq `8f6c`, CLOSED):** Layer-1 does NOT add its own tool integrations; it calls the code-grounding evidence oracle (`src/rebar/grounding/`, the `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan` facade) delivered by the now-closed prerequisite epic `8f6c`. The oracle supplies, polyglot + fail-open: symbol/import/file resolution (T1, universal-ctags), package existence (T0, deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM), and size/smell scans (Engine B — OpenGrep/ast-grep + scc/lizard). Its three-valued, abstain-by-default contract IS the fail-open guarantee (unsupported lang / no tool / parse-error / timeout → `abstain` with a recorded reason → skipped = PASS, never FAIL; the recorded abstain set IS the coverage). LSP/`solidlsp` semantic resolution is the T2 follow-on (epic `850f`, open), NOT v1.
- **Execution (exec-tier-aware):** DET criteria run as parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN criteria are the only
  chunked tier — packed by `facet` at `base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket)` (config-overridable; default Opus
  ~all-in-one cached call, Sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, halved for large/epic/has_children). AGENT (tool-using)
  criteria run ONE agent each (≈85× a single-turn call) and are gated by proportionate scrutiny. Prompt caching on
  (system+plan prefix) makes fine chunking cheap. Depth scales with complexity + has_children; escalation by
  size + complexity + centrality (computed at plan time). Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing;
  INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first. Defaults grounded by experiment (see below).
- **Proportionate scrutiny (by LEVEL + TYPE; experiment-grounded round 4):** criteria carry an `applies_at` field; the orchestrator filters before chunking so leaf-implementation criteria (E6 proving-commands, G1G2/E4/A1, leaf overlays T5a/b/c) do NOT run at epic/story altitude, container criteria (G3/G4 child coverage) run only when has_children, and TYPE rules apply (bugs EXEMPT→follow-on; E5 suppressed on RED/GREEN test tasks; F4 N/A on internal/mechanical leaf tasks). Without this the gate over-fires on mid-level stories and mechanical tasks.
- **Overlay triggering (deterministic-where-low-FP, else LLM-router; validated round 4):** T1 prior-art, T5a performance, and T5d accessibility fire from low-FP deterministic signals (bright-lines / UI-keyword count); T8 LLM-antipatterns, T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state are LLM-routed (a keyword trigger is high-FP for them — e.g. T8 keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket). T8 is in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans (it recovers structural-gap signal a generic checklist misses). FINALIZE CORRECTION (E4-confirmed): the new resource overlays T10/T11/T12 are LLM-ROUTED for PLAN review, NOT deterministic — on a held-out non-DSO corpus the deterministic keyword over-fired (rebar 'migration' = the bash→Python strangler, not a data-migration: det T11 fired 2/19 plans, all false; T12 'deploy'/'rollback' fired 7/19, all false; the LLM router fired 0). On a code-review DIFF the file-glob triggers (the 9da1 overlay catalog) are high-precision; on a PLAN there is no diff, so route by LLM.
- **Attestation:** signed result = "process followed to the best of our ability, no red flags + coverage" — NOT
  "perfect". Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition edits invalidate;
  tags/comments/links/assignee do not). The cert is the composable CI rigor signal.
- **Observability / FP substrate (cheap, in-scope):** a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR `REVIEW_RESULT` event on the
  ticket (out of hot paths) capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + override
  justifications; the signature stays a normal head-state event. Offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints +
  existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) yields per-criterion fire/remediation/FP/FN rates. Capture
  only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- **Bugs are SKIPPED** in this epic (different shape; dedicated follow-on).

## Review structure — three-pass (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate)

Adopted from epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b` (the shared three-pass framework for all rebar reviews) + the
code-review plan-of-record (session log `9dba-8e84-0d0e-4af8`). It **replaces per-criterion model-emitted
severity/confidence**: LLMs are unreliable at holistic severity/confidence, so neither is emitted by the model
anywhere in the decision path. The criteria registry below is the LOCKED RUBRIC the Pass-1 findings reference;
each descriptor's prose `SEVERITY:` text is now a PRIOR for the Pass-2 attribute assessment, not a model output.

- **Pass 1 — find (evidence records):** the reviewer surfaces FINDINGS as
  `{finding, criteria[] (rubric ids), evidence[] (flexible: plan quote / section / ABSENCE rationale / code
  citation), scenarios[], impact}` — NO severity, NO confidence. Run per facet-chunk of the rubric (the chunking
  defaults still apply); PASS = no finding for a criterion; an empty list for a clean chunk is expected.
- **Pass 2 — verify (independent):** a SEPARATE verifier agent, fresh context, re-grounds in the plan (and the
  code AGENTICALLY for codebase-grounded findings) and emits per finding (a) severity ATTRIBUTES
  `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` (coarse ordinals) and (b) typed BINARY
  sub-answers `{verdict: yes|no|insufficient}`. Rules: atomicity (one proposition/question); INDEPENDENCE (the
  finding is presented as a CLAIM TO TEST, its conclusion not asserted); verdict-with-citation NOT verdict-with-fix;
  `insufficient` allowed.
- **Pass 3 — decide (deterministic):** the ONLY veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a finding
  cites a specific code reference (a no-op for the non-citable absence findings that dominate plan review).
  confidence (= VALIDITY) = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers (yes=1, insufficient=.5, no=0) ∈ [0,1];
  IMPACT ∈ [0,1] = the deterministic mean of the Pass-2 severity attributes, each ordinal mapped to [0,1]
  (prod_impact/debt_impact none/low/medium/high → 0/.33/.67/1, take the max of the two; blast_radius local/module/
  system → .33/.67/1; likelihood low/med/high → .33/.67/1; reversibility easy/moderate/hard → .33/.67/1); the
  severity LABEL is the same attributes bucketed (display only). UNIFIED PRIORITY SCORE = validity × impact ∈ [0,1]
  — the ONE number driving both the block threshold and the advisory ranking (this attribute→[0,1] map + the
  product are the v1 DEFAULT shape; weights are calibration-tunable). decision = `block | advisory | dropped`: DROP
  if validity < .5; else BLOCK iff (not cited-reference-vetoed) AND priority ≥ the criterion's `block_threshold`
  (default 0.95 ⇒ near-certain AND high-impact, so v1 is almost all advisory); else ADVISORY.  an LLM-tier criterion DOES block when a project opts it in / lowers its threshold; blocking is not DET-only. No model holistic severity/confidence in the path; no interactive
  escalation (autonomous).
- **Pass 4 — coach (advisory; AFTER the gate, NOT in the decision path):** a single-turn structured-output
  call over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings that maps each to a move from the registry and renders coaching
  from a locked template — priming the caller's next revision; it NEVER alters the Pass-3 decision (see the
  Design summary + child `75a9`).
- **Pass-2 vocabulary (validated against `criteria_v8`).** Severity ATTRIBUTES: `prod_impact` / `debt_impact` ∈ {none, low, medium, high}; `blast_radius` ∈ {local, module, system}; `likelihood` ∈ {low, medium, high}; `reversibility` ∈ {easy, moderate, hard}. BINARY sub-answers ∈ {yes, no, insufficient}: the VETO `cited_reference_accurate` (answered only when a code citation is present, else `na`) + the graded set {`is_verifiable`, `evidence_entails_finding`, `path_reachable`, `impact_follows_necessarily`, `no_viable_alternative_explanation`, `no_existing_mitigation`, `severity_claim_justified`}. Pass-3 maps the attributes → severity and the graded fraction → confidence.
- **Binary sub-question set (typed):** veto = cited-reference-accuracy; graded = is-verifiable,
  evidence-entails-finding, path-reachable, impact-follows-necessarily, no-viable-alternative-explanation,
  no-existing-mitigation, severity-claim-justified. (Research: Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE,
  RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK — binary decomposition + deterministic aggregation beats a single LLM confidence number.)
- **Plan-review specialization:** evidence is flexible precisely because plan findings are often NON-citable
  (an absence finding like “the plan never specifies X” has no line to cite); the cited-reference veto is therefore
  a no-op for them and fires only when a finding does cite code.

The three-pass-native descriptor set is `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v8.json`
(`severity_by=pass3`, `default_posture=advisory`, `block_threshold`, `checklist[]`, declarative `applies_at`).

## Criteria registry (authoritative full set — v4 §5; canonical)

The complete criteria set. This list is the authority; the experiment subsets are not. A completeness guard
(`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/check_registry_coverage.py`) fails loudly if any criterion here is
missing from the implemented set, so subsetting can never silently drop one again.

**Layer 1 — DET (deterministic, blocks, fail-open):** P1 readiness-shape (`## Acceptance Criteria`, all types) ·
P2 file/symbol/import resolution (via the 8f6c oracle: universal-ctags T1; LSP/solidlsp = T2/`850f`) · P3 package existence (via the 8f6c oracle: deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM) · P4 oversize
signals · P5 task-DAG validity + cross-task interference · P6 AC/DD quality (compound-AND, vague-lexicon,
spec-fidelity, verify-command robustness) · P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates to T4) · P8 reviewability/context-budget (FAILS only when the ticket — or, for container criteria, a parent+child pairing — exceeds, or is estimated to exceed, the largest context window even at one-criterion-per-call: 'too big to review in full, reduce/decompose it'; the extreme of P4 oversize / G5).

**Layer 2 — judgment (advisory; single-turn / 2-step / AGENT):** F1 measurability & in-session completability ·
F4 user/problem present · E1 criteria↔description coherence + terminology + duplicates · E2 ambiguity +
undercooked + decision-deferral · E3 intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate) · E5 testing-plan completeness ·
E6 verification/termination + end-state-reachability · G5 decomposition judgment · G1+G2 edit-set/scope accuracy
(AGENT) · E4 assumption/premise verification (AGENT) · A1 anti-slop/NIH + the 7 anti-patterns (AGENT) · G3 child coverage (AGENT, container-only) · G4 child consistency (AGENT, container-only) · G6 approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection (AGENT; mechanism-correctness + best-approach check, with reviewer-generated alternatives so no rejected options reach the implementer) · ISF intent-source fidelity (single-turn/2-STEP, FED the linked session log + ticket graph, NOT agent; fires ONLY when the ticket is linked to a session log; catches silent descoping of an externally-expressed requirement). (Final AGENT tier = E4/G1G2/A1/G6/T8/T1/T3/T5c/T10/T11 — settled by the grounding experiment: a verdict that depends on WHAT THE ACTUAL CODE DOES is agent-tier (T5c security, T10 infra found a real committed secret, T11 migration by analogy/untested). Single-turn implication overlays whose verdict is plan-text-evident: T5a/T5b/T5e/T9/T4 (T5a/T5b/T11 flagged for eval re-test). G3/G4 are AGENT-tier container-only criteria (per `criteria_v8` + the AGENT-tier tool-access contract below: they read the live ticket graph).)

**Triggered overlays (deterministic where low-FP — T1/T5a/T5d; else LLM-routed — T8/T6/T5b/T5c/T9):** T1
prior-art/novel-architecture · T2 empirical probe (red→green / spike) · T3 integration feasibility · T4
compat/destructiveness-as-explicit-choice · T5a performance · T5b reliability · T5c security · T5d accessibility ·
